name: Luncheon Oyster Cups
recipeInstructions:
Melt the margarine and add three tablespoons of flour. Cook until the mixture bubbles. Gradually add one-and-a-half cups of cold milk, stirring constantly. Stir in the celery and cook for twenty minutes in a double boiler. Add the chopped pimiento and cheese.
Cook the oysters in their own liquor until the edges curl. Add them cut into small pieces, along with the strained liquor, to the white sauce.
To make the cup bases: Sift together two cups of flour, salt, and baking powder. Mix in the shortening thoroughly, then add about three-fourths cup of milk or enough to form a soft dough. Toss on a well-floured board, roll to one-half inch thickness, and cut with a biscuit cutter.
Press each biscuit into an inverted greased gem pan. Bake until lightly browned in a 450°F oven. Arrange on a hot platter and fill the cups with oyster mixture.
